steps.title: Set up the conversion ratio between degrees and radians
steps.content:
The fundamental relationship between degrees and radians is:

$$180° = \pi \text{ radians}$$

To convert any angle in degrees to radians, multiply by the conversion factor $\frac{\pi}{180}$.

steps.title: Apply the conversion formula to 45 degrees
steps.content:
Multiply 45° by the conversion factor:

$$45° \times \frac{\pi}{180} = \frac{45\pi}{180}$$

Simplify by dividing both numerator and denominator by 45:

$$\frac{45\pi}{180} = \frac{\pi}{4} = \frac{1}{4}\pi$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
